APPLICATION DETAILS.Scientific Abstract:
Please use this section to provide an overview of your planned application for NIHR career development funding. This should be tailored to the career development scheme you intend to apply to and cover:
the area of research that you are interested in developing your knowledge in
an outline of the research proposal (if appropriate)
the training and development plan and how this will support your career development
how patient and public involvement will be incorporated into the application for funding.
{1100 words!!]
APPLICATION DETAILS.Working with People and Communities Summ:
For my CDRF, through a Community-Based Participatory Research approach, young people and companions from the TYA cancer community became co-researchers and contributed to every stage of the research process.
I will reconvene this Community-of-Inquiry to help prioritise the research questions and study design for the ACAF research. I will invite three co-researchers who were part of the CDRF research to continue their collaboration with me, with an additional five new patients and family members. I will also draw on my ongoing collaboration and commitment of charities Teenage Cancer Trust and Young Lives vs Cancer, alongside the Children’s Cancer Leukaemia Group and my research support team. Patient and public involvement and diversity of inclusion will be incorporated into the developing ACAF research from the outset.

SUMMARY BUDGET:
Costs must be provided at current prices. An adjustment for inflation will be made annually thereafter at rates set by DHSC. Whilst allowances for incremental increases should be included on the form, nationally or locally agreed pay increases should be excluded.

III) Training and Development. Please itemise and describe fully the costs associated with training and development. Please provide estimates if exact costs are not available at the time of application. Any travel and subsistence associated with training and development should not be included here and should be included in the travel section of the form.

IV) Other Costs. Additional support required to submit an application that extends beyond the other cost
headings available can be requested here. A clear justification and breakdown for how costs have been calculated should be provided. Costs must be provided at current prices.
It is expected the total value of other costs requested will not exceed £20,000. All support requested will need to be additional costs directly associated with preparing an application for an NIHR career development scheme.

Co-Applicants:
Please enter the details of up to 4 individuals who will have oversight and give direction to your proposed application (if applicable).
The individuals you list here must also be added in the Participants and Signatories section of the application form. Although we acknowledge that formal supervision may not be appropriate for applicants, we believe that the application will benefit from research support or mentorship. The individuals who provide research support may or may not be based in your host organisation.
Research support is referred to in the literature as mentorship and there are numerous models to be found that could be employed. Clearly describe how the proposed arrangements will support your overall development and provide an initial assessment of the time that will be allocated to the research support process.
With an emphasis on broadening my networks, and to provide a strong academic and clinical environment beyond my current institution, PhD supervisory panel and pre-existing support networks,  I will benefit from the support of four established academics, whose expertise aligns in intersectional and complementary ways. Each mentor has committed to a proposed arrangement whereby we will meet individually each quarter, and on a whole-team basis on at least two occasions during the 12-month period.
During this time, I will be supported to determine the most suitable academic partner, faculty and department for my proposed ACAF research. More frequent meetings are envisaged from month 6 among research support mentors who may become support participants in my ACAF application.
Dr Georgia Black Georgia Black - Wolfson Institute of Population Health (qmul.ac.uk)
Dr Helen Hatcher Dr Helen Hatcher (crukcambridgecentre.org.uk) (8) Helen Hatcher (researchgate.net)
Prof Joanne McPeake Professor Joanne McPeake - THIS Institute - The Healthcare Improvement Studies Institute (cam.ac.uk)
Dr Nicole Brown https://profiles.ucl.ac.uk/48702-nicole-brown

headings available can be requested here. A clear justification and breakdown for how costs have been calculated should be provided. Costs must be provided at current prices.
It is expected the total value of other costs requested will not exceed £20,000. All support requested will need to be additional costs directly associated with preparing an application for an NIHR career development scheme.
LEAD APPLICANT & RESEARCH TEAM.Host Organisation:
The statement is completed by the host organisation Head of Department and should detail how the organisation is going to support the applicant to prepare their application for NIHR career development funding.
Demonstrate track record of the host department in training and supporting people who have gone on to develop successful academic careers.

methods.text:
In 2011, the English National Health Service (NHS) introduced Ambulatory Care (AC) for Teenage and Young Adult (TYA) cancer patients, offering chemotherapy and treatment that traditionally require hospitalisation on an ambulatory basis. This treatment modality is made possible using portable infusion pumps, with young people residing close to the hospital overnight, and engaged in self-surveillance and self-monitoring aspects of care.
My CDRF research set out to explore the experiences of those both receiving and delivering AC: building knowledge to inform current and future services for those aged 16-24 and their families. A key aim of this project was to understand whether refinements to the service were needed to better support young people and their companions at the study site and nationally, commensurate with growing interest in this care pathway. The research has realised many impacts, including….[move content from the first section here?)
For my CDRF, through a Community-Based Participatory Research approach, young people and companions from the TYA cancer community became co-researchers and contributed to every stage of the research process.
I will reconvene this Community-of-Inquiry to help prioritise the research questions and study design for the ACAF research. I will invite three co-researchers who were part of the CDRF research to continue their collaboration with me, with an additional five new patients and family members. I will also draw on my ongoing collaboration and commitment of charities Teenage Cancer Trust and Young Lives vs Cancer, alongside the Children’s Cancer Leukaemia Group and my research support team. Patient and public involvement and diversity of inclusion will be incorporated into the developing ACAF research from the outset.

overall_plan.text:
Having most recently completed JBI scoping reviews, I would access the free modules on undertaking systematic reviews from: Cochrane Interactive Learning | Cochrane Training. Once the research question has been refined, a systematic review will become the basis for the development of the study.
This short course, Building & Using a Theory of Change (the-sra.org.uk) will enable me to appraise, at an early stage whether ‘theory building’ could be a constituent part of the planned research.
In the first 4 months of the Fund, I would draw on the expertise of my research support mentors to help me identify senior participants for my ACAF application who provide the best possible fit for my project.
This application also includes travel and subsistence to enable me to participate in the 6th Global Adolescent and Young Adult Cancer Congress (eventsair.com), in December 2024 in Melbourne. This is the only worldwide network that is focused on improving cancer care and outcomes for young people, and where international academics in the field convene.
Participatory research by nature, promotes the communication of a project from its development, to maximise interest and subsequent impact. To further enhance the public engagement component of how I communicate research across different media, I have identified this short course: Science Communication: Masterclass - Professional/Short course - UWE Bristol: Courses
This aspect of my development would form part of my mentoring relationship with Professor Joanne McPeake. I would, in addition, like to apply for an NIHR executive leadership coaching session, knowing this opportunity has been well-evaluated among peers navigating clinical-academic careers within an NHS leadership role.
I have costed an additional £300 into this grant, to enable me to access the expertise of a subject matter or methodological expert and research leader, should this developmental need be identified during the 12-months.

research_support.text:
With an emphasis on broadening my networks, and to provide a strong academic and clinical environment beyond my current institution, PhD supervisory panel and pre-existing support networks, I will benefit from the support of four established academics, whose expertise aligns in intersectional and complementary ways. Each mentor has committed to a proposed arrangement whereby we will meet individually each quarter, and on a whole-team basis on at least two occasions during the 12-month period.
During this time, I will be supported to determine the most suitable academic partner, faculty and department for my proposed ACAF research. More frequent meetings are envisaged from month 6 among research support mentors who may become support participants in my ACAF application.
Dr Georgia Black Georgia Black - Wolfson Institute of Population Health (qmul.ac.uk)
Dr Helen Hatcher Dr Helen Hatcher (crukcambridgecentre.org.uk) (8) Helen Hatcher (researchgate.net)
Prof Joanne McPeake Professor Joanne McPeake - THIS Institute - The Healthcare Improvement Studies Institute (cam.ac.uk)
Dr Nicole Brown https://profiles.ucl.ac.uk/48702-nicole-brown
